hypothesis.strategy: Add 4-5 new 2026 papers each to the 3 lowest-scoring articles, plus 1 SVG diagram each, targeting sourcing, currency, and depth dimensions
hypothesis.reasoning: active-learning has sourcing=62 (lowest dimension), computational-cost has depth=58, and transfer-learning has currency=66. Past sessions show 4-5 new 2026 papers per article reliably yields +6-9 per article. Adding SVG diagrams boosts depth. These are the 3 lowest-scoring articles so improving them will maximize composite gains.

result.lesson: All 3 articles gained 5.9-8.8 points. active-learning had the biggest jump (+8.8) because sourcing went 62->85 and depth 70->90 with new sections on GFlowNets, info-theoretic foundations, and budget-constrained batch AL plus a decision tree diagram. computational-cost depth improved 58->76 with EcoThink, cost trajectories, and adaptive compute sections. transfer-learning currency jumped 66->87 with 5 new 2025-2026 papers on continual learning, domain adaptation, and sim-to-real transfer.
